raw_response:
Adeno ameloblastoma typically arises from the jaw bones and can involve adjacent tissues. Treatment generally aims to remove the tumor en bloc along with adequate margins to prevent recurrence. Given the characteristics of adeno ameloblastoma, the best approach would likely be:
\boxed{A} Enbloc resection of maxilla To treat adeno ameloblastoma effectively, removing the tumor en bloc with sufficient margins is crucial to reduce the risk of recurrence. Therefore, \boxed{A} Enbloc resection of maxilla appears to be the optimal treatment method among the given options. Options B, C, and D do not adequately ensure complete removal of the tumor and may increase the likelihood of residual disease.
